title: quinquina succirubra
intro: Quinquina succirubra, also known as Cinchona succirubra, is a flowering plant native to the Andes mountains in South America, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of quinine, a compound that has been used to treat malaria and other febrile conditions. The health benefits of Quinquina succirubra include its anti-inflammatory and antimalarial properties, which have been used to alleviate symptoms such as fever and joint pain. The herbal preparations of the plant include dried bark, which is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
